class.name: CoiledAnchor
class.brief_description: A [RopeAnchor] that generates positions arranged in a coil/helix pattern.
class.description: CoiledAnchor produces a series of anchor positions wound in a helix around the local X axis. It is useful for representing rope wound around a drum, winch, or spool. The coil geometry is controlled by [member radius], [member turns_per_layer], and [member clockwise]. The number of generated positions depends on [member coiled] (the length of rope that is coiled) and [member particles_per_meter].

members.member: If [code]true[/code], the coil winds clockwise when viewed from the positive X axis. Otherwise it winds counter-clockwise.
The radius of the first layer of the coil, in meters.
The number of full turns that fit in each layer before the coil advances to the next layer.
An angular offset applied to each coil turn, in turns.
The length of rope (in meters) that the coil represents. Together with [member particles_per_meter] this determines how many anchor positions are generated.
The number of anchor positions to generate per meter of coiled rope.
The width of the rope, used to compute layer and turn spacing within the coil.
